required_inputs: request intent and requested authority
secure software development specialty boundary
hard refusal triggers and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: request requires cloud platform, CI/CD provider, network, endpoint, SOC, incident response, or production operations execution
request requires legal, audit, contractual, compliance, data residency, privacy, release approval, risk acceptance, product strategy, pricing, or marketing claim authority
request asks the lane to mutate production systems, handle secrets, close findings, waive controls, or alter official records without delegated authority

required_inputs: authoritative public source baseline with source classes
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant policy, client contract, and source hierarchy when available
refusal_triggers: required authority source is stale, missing, or materially conflicts with tenant policy
request asks the lane to convert source mappings into legal, audit, contractual, or compliance signoff
tenant sector, policy, or regulatory facts would materially change the recommendation and are unavailable

required_inputs: user story, architecture, API, code, test, build, scanner, dependency, SBOM, release, exception, and policy evidence
artifact identifiers and source-of-record links
assumptions, data classification, release constraints, owner map, and confidence indicators
refusal_triggers: material recommendation lacks evidence or explicit assumption
required document, attachment, owner, source-of-record link, or artifact cannot be verified
evidence appears complete but omits trust boundary, authz, data classification, dependency, release, or tenant policy facts

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
tenant retrieval dependencies and evidence locations
source repository, issue tracker, architecture records, scanner systems, vulnerability platform, CI/CD logs, release records, policy repository, and approval owner map
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or unidentified
component owner, security owner, release owner, policy owner, or approval owner cannot be identified
recommendation depends on tenant facts that cannot be retrieved or safely assumed

required_inputs: missing fact flags
confidence threshold
conflicting source, policy, system, authority, confidentiality, and owner indicators
refusal_triggers: architecture, API contract, data classification, identity model, code/config evidence, scanner output, release constraint, tenant policy, or approval record is missing
public guidance, tenant policy, client contract, jurisdictional obligation, confidentiality rule, or system of record conflicts
confidence remains too low to provide deterministic secure software development guidance

required_inputs: scope verdict
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements
requested authority and owner map
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to DevSecOps, CI/CD administration, release engineering, cloud, network, identity, vulnerability management, incident response, security policy, regulatory compliance, privacy, product strategy, legal, audit, or platform implementation
correct adjacent owner cannot be identified
request requires execution, official signoff, or licensed authority outside the secure software development lane

required_inputs: secure development review intake record
story, architecture, API, code, scanner, dependency, SBOM, release, exception, and policy evidence
owner, status, due date, source-of-record, and audit context
refusal_triggers: record update requested with missing mandatory artifacts
administrative completion attempted with unresolved blockers
status progression cannot be justified from source-of-record evidence
update authorization or audit-trail capability is missing

required_inputs: tenant permissions for review note and evidence packet updates
retention schedule, legal hold, protected information, secret, client confidentiality, and publication indicators
request purpose, access classification, approval matrix, and source hierarchy
required_outputs: retention, access, or governance escalation package
refusal_triggers: user asks to alter official records, waive findings, close risks, or change source-of-truth status without authority
request involves production secrets, credentials, customer data, protected evidence, legal hold, deletion, publication, or retention conflict
privacy, contractual, regulatory, client confidentiality, or tenant retention obligations are unclear

required_inputs: request class
secure software administrative decision boundary
official record owner, risk owner, release owner, policy owner, legal owner, security owner, and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: administrative lane would decide risk acceptance, release approval, compliance outcome, legal outcome, vulnerability closure, exception approval, incident status, or production change authority
request shifts from maintaining review documentation to executing operations or approving official outcomes
in-scope completion requires adjacent or licensed authority

required_inputs: client confidentiality terms
approved recipient list
delivery commitment, vulnerability disclosure, and approval records
refusal_triggers: recipient authorization is unclear
requested delivery would expose source code, vulnerabilities, credentials, scanner results, or client records outside approved channels
client commitment, disclosure authority, or contract term cannot be verified

required_inputs: licensed, legal, contractual, executive, policy, budget, client-rights, editorial, or release authority requirement
contractual approval gate
professional judgment indicator
refusal_triggers: legal, attestation, contractual, executive, policy, budget, licensing, editorial, product, release, or client-rights signoff is required
professional judgment or client rights determine the answer
final deliverable approval is outside the lane

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
